scenario: Sable Logistics modernizes a Camunda 8.8 worker fleet that handles 13000 shipments per day and still configures camunda.client.zeebe.grpc-address in its application.yaml. The platform team must rename the property to the documented 8.8 replacement before the next release.
question: Which property does the 8.8 reference list as the replacement for the legacy camunda.client.zeebe.grpc-address key?

options.text: The 8.8 reference replaces camunda.client.zeebe.grpc-address with camunda.client.grpc-address under the unified camunda.client section of the Spring Boot starter.
The 8.8 reference replaces camunda.client.zeebe.grpc-address with camunda.client.cloud.grpc-address under the unified camunda.client section of the Spring Boot starter.
The 8.8 reference replaces camunda.client.zeebe.grpc-address with camunda.client.rest-address under the unified camunda.client section of the Spring Boot starter.
The 8.8 reference replaces camunda.client.zeebe.grpc-address with camunda.client.broker.grpc-address under the unified camunda.client section of the Spring Boot starter.

a.text: Correct. The camunda.client.zeebe table points camunda.client.zeebe.grpc-address to camunda.client.grpc-address as the documented 8.8 replacement key.

b.text: Incorrect. The camunda.client.cloud section does not list a grpc-address entry; the replacement is camunda.client.grpc-address at the unified level.

c.text: Incorrect. rest-address is the replacement for camunda.client.zeebe.rest-address; the gRPC endpoint maps to camunda.client.grpc-address.

d.text: Incorrect. The unified key drops the broker segment; the replacement listed in the 8.8 reference is camunda.client.grpc-address.

explanation: camunda.client.zeebe.grpc-address migrates to camunda.client.grpc-address in the 8.8 reference. The unified camunda.client section exposes one gRPC endpoint for all interactions previously routed through the zeebe subsection.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
